name: São Tomé and Príncipe Portuguese

ancestors.notes: European Portuguese colonial base. São Tomé and Príncipe was uninhabited before Portuguese settlement from 1486; among the oldest Portuguese colonial societies.

Angolan Portuguese adstrate: post-independence immigration from Angola; also, São Tomense (Forro), Angolar, and Principense are Portuguese-based creoles native to the islands — modelled here via Angolan Portuguese as the nearest registry code.
Angolan Portuguese adstrate: post-independence immigration from Angola brought Angolan Portuguese patterns to São Tomé.

notes: São Tomé and Príncipe Portuguese (Português de São Tomé e Príncipe). Official language; increasingly replacing the traditional creoles (Forro/São Tomense, Angolar, Principense) as L1 in younger generations. Unique as one of the few places where Portuguese is genuinely replacing endemic creoles. KEY FEATURES: (1) FIVE-VOWEL SYSTEM: no /ɨ/; full unstressed vowels as in other African varieties. (2) SIBILANTS: coda /s/ → [s], not palatalised. (3) RHOTIC: alveolar [r]. (4) OPEN VOWELS: /e/, /o/ tend to open realisations. (5) HISTORICAL DEPTH: São Tomé received enslaved Africans from across the Gulf of Guinea from the 15th century, making its creoles among the oldest Atlantic creoles; this unique history shapes the Portuguese spoken there. Sources: Ferraz (1979), Günther (1973), Hagemeijer (2009).

sources.author: Ferraz, L.I.
sources.title: The Creole of São Tomé
sources.notes: Key reference for São Tomense creole and Portuguese relationship

sources.author: Hagemeijer, T.
sources.title: The Gulf of Guinea creoles: genetic and typological relations
sources.publisher: Journal of Pidgin and Creole Languages

sources.author: Gonçalves, P.
sources.title: Português de Moçambique: uma variedade em formação

allophone_rules.notes: São Tomé Portuguese keeps an alveolar coda /s/ (no Lisbon 'chiado'); this overrides the inherited pt-PT PT_CODA_S_HUSH rule by id, matching the alveolar coda already declared in this spec's positional_graphemes.
São Tomé Portuguese keeps an alveolar coda /z/; overrides the inherited pt-PT PT_CODA_Z_HUSH rule by id.

DISABLED for this variety. The EP Lisbon-standard centralisation of the word-final ⟨-em⟩ nucleus to [ɐ̃] (inherited from pt-PT) does not apply here: this variety keeps the close-mid nasal nucleus [ẽ], so ⟨-em⟩ is [ẽj̃] (glide via PT_FINAL_EM_GLIDE), not [ɐ̃j̃]. Peripheral/non-Iberian Portuguese does not follow the Lisbon centralisation.

stress.notes: Acordo Ortográfico 1990 conventions: paroxytone default; written accents override; final -i/-u/-r/-l/-z/-x/-im/-om/-um/-ns and nasal diphthongs attract final stress. Unmarked -em/-am stay paroxytone (homem, falam). DIPHTHONGS: declared so the bundled syllabifier counts nuclei correctly — without them every vowel run merges into one nucleus and co-e-lho is read as coe-lho, one syllable short, putting the stress and its vowel reduction on the wrong vowel. ⟨oe⟩ is hiatus, not a diphthong (Cunha & Cintra, §Ditongos).

location.notes: the city of São Tomé. Note the spec's wikidata_qid (Q36536) is Forro CREOLE, a different language from this Portuguese variety, so it was NOT used as the source of this point. [Wikidata Q3932 P625]
